seoTitle: How to Receive a Purchase Order in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to receive a purchase order in QuickBooks Online so incoming inventory and purchases are tracked accurately against what you ordered.
intro: Once goods arrive from a supplier, marking the purchase order as received keeps your inventory and purchasing records in sync. This guide walks through the steps to receive a purchase order in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Open the purchase order
steps.body: Find the purchase order you placed with the vendor for the items that have now arrived.

steps.heading: Mark items as received
steps.body: Select the receive option and confirm the quantities that actually arrived, which may differ from what was ordered.

steps.heading: Handle partial receipts if needed
steps.body: If only some items arrived, mark just those as received and leave the rest open on the purchase order.

steps.heading: Convert to a bill
steps.body: Once received, convert the purchase order into a bill so the payment obligation is tracked.

faq.question: What happens if I receive fewer items than ordered?
faq.answer: You can mark a partial quantity as received, and the purchase order will remain open for the remaining items.

faq.question: Does receiving a purchase order update my inventory automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, receiving inventory items updates your on-hand quantities in QuickBooks Online.

faq.question: Do I need to receive the order before paying the bill?
faq.answer: It's best practice to receive the goods first, then convert to a bill and process payment, to keep quantities accurate.

faq.question: Can I edit a purchase order after items are received?
faq.answer: You can make adjustments, but changes after receiving may require additional corrections to keep quantities and costs aligned.
